questions.q: The Rashtrakutas are most famous for building which rock-cut temple at Ellora?
questions.explanation: The Rashtrakutas are celebrated for the monolithic Kailasa (Kailasanatha) temple at Ellora.

questions.q: The Rashtrakutas were a powerful dynasty of which region of India?
questions.explanation: The Rashtrakutas ruled the Deccan plateau and parts of central and southern India.

questions.q: During roughly which centuries did the imperial Rashtrakutas hold power?
questions.explanation: The imperial Rashtrakutas flourished from about the 8th to the 10th centuries CE.

questions.q: The Kailasa temple at Ellora is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple is a grand shrine dedicated to Shiva, evoking his mountain abode Mount Kailasa.

questions.q: The Kailasa temple is remarkable because it was created by what method?
options: Carved from a single rock from the top down
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple was carved out of a single basalt cliff, excavated downward from the top.

questions.q: The Rashtrakutas were notable patrons of literature in how many languages?
questions.explanation: Rashtrakuta courts patronised works in several languages, notably Kannada, Sanskrit, and Prakrit.

questions.q: The Rashtrakutas frequently contended for control of which northern Indian imperial city?
questions.explanation: The Rashtrakutas were one of the three great powers fighting for the city of Kannauj.

questions.q: Ellora, the site of the Kailasa temple, is located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Ellora caves and the Kailasa temple are in present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: Which of these was a hallmark of Rashtrakuta achievement?
questions.explanation: The Rashtrakutas are remembered for their lavish patronage of art, literature, and temple architecture.

questions.q: The Rashtrakutas are described as a major power of which broad era?
questions.explanation: They were a leading power of early medieval India during the 8th to 10th centuries CE.

questions.q: Who is generally regarded as the founder of the imperial Rashtrakuta dynasty?
questions.explanation: Dantidurga overthrew the Chalukyas of Badami and established imperial Rashtrakuta power around the mid-8th century.

questions.q: Which Rashtrakuta king is credited with commissioning the Kailasa temple at Ellora?
questions.explanation: The Kailasa temple is attributed to Krishna I in the 8th century CE.

questions.q: Which long-reigning Rashtrakuta king authored the Kannada classic Kavirajamarga?
questions.explanation: Amoghavarsha I is associated with the Kavirajamarga, an early landmark of Kannada literature.

questions.q: Amoghavarsha I founded which city, which became a Rashtrakuta capital?
questions.explanation: Amoghavarsha I established Manyakheta (modern Malkhed) as the Rashtrakuta capital.

questions.q: Which Rashtrakuta king led a successful military campaign deep into northern India and is said to have reached Kannauj?
questions.explanation: Govinda III conducted far-reaching northern campaigns, defeating the Pratiharas and Palas.

questions.q: The Arab traveller Al-Masudi praised a Rashtrakuta king as one of the great rulers of the world; whom did he call by the title 'Balhara'?
questions.explanation: Arab writers called the Rashtrakuta ruler 'Balhara', a rendering of the title Vallabharaja, ranking him among the world's foremost kings.

questions.q: Which Rashtrakuta king defeated the Cholas at the Battle of Takkolam in the 10th century?
questions.explanation: Krishna III defeated the Cholas at Takkolam and extended Rashtrakuta influence into the Tamil country.

questions.q: Which dynasty did the Rashtrakutas displace when they rose to imperial power in the Deccan?
questions.explanation: Dantidurga overthrew the Chalukyas of Badami (Vatapi) to found Rashtrakuta supremacy.

questions.q: Apart from Hinduism, the Rashtrakutas were notable patrons of which religion, reflected in some Ellora caves?
questions.explanation: The Rashtrakutas patronised Jainism alongside Hindu traditions, evident in Jain caves at Ellora.

questions.q: Which dynasty finally overthrew the Rashtrakutas, ending their imperial rule around 973 CE?
questions.explanation: Tailapa II of the Western (Kalyani) Chalukyas overthrew the last Rashtrakuta around 973 CE.

questions.q: The early Rashtrakutas, before becoming an imperial power, ruled as feudatories under which Deccan overlords?
questions.explanation: Before Dantidurga's rise, the Rashtrakutas served as subordinate chiefs under the Chalukyas of Badami in the Deccan.

questions.q: The Sanskrit poet Jinasena, author of the Adipurana, was associated with the court of which Rashtrakuta king?
questions.explanation: The Jain scholar Jinasena was a preceptor connected with Amoghavarsha I's court.

questions.q: Which Rashtrakuta king sacked the Pratihara capital Kannauj in the early 10th century?
questions.explanation: Indra III famously raided and devastated Kannauj around 916 CE during the struggle with the Pratiharas.

questions.q: The Kannada mathematician Mahaviracharya, author of the Ganita-sara-sangraha, lived under which Rashtrakuta ruler?
questions.explanation: The mathematician Mahaviracharya flourished under the patronage of Amoghavarsha I.

questions.q: Who was the last significant Rashtrakuta emperor, overthrown by Tailapa II?
questions.explanation: Karka II was the last Rashtrakuta ruler, deposed by the Chalukya Tailapa II around 973 CE.

questions.q: The Sanjan and Nilgund copper-plate inscriptions are important sources for which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Sanjan and Nilgund plates are key epigraphic records of Rashtrakuta history and genealogy.

questions.q: Govinda III decisively defeated which Pratihara king during his northern campaign?
questions.explanation: Govinda III defeated the Pratihara Nagabhata II in his ambitious northern expedition.

questions.q: The Rashtrakuta capital Manyakheta corresponds to which modern town in Karnataka?
questions.explanation: Manyakheta is identified with the modern town of Malkhed in Karnataka.

questions.q: Amoghavarsha I, devoted and contemplative, is traditionally said to have repeatedly done what during his reign?
options: Abdicated or withdrawn from rule temporarily for religious pursuits
Led naval invasions of Southeast Asia
questions.explanation: Amoghavarsha I, inclined toward Jainism and learning, is said to have set aside the throne at times for religious life.

questions.q: The name 'Rashtrakuta' is generally understood to derive from a term denoting what?
options: Heads or chiefs of a rashtra (territorial division)
questions.explanation: Rashtrakuta derives from terms meaning chiefs or officers in charge of a rashtra, a territorial or administrative division.
